headline: Euler V2 discovery incomplete — addresses dynamically loaded; 16 chains identified; core repos and audit firms catalogued
short_headline: Addresses loaded dynamically; core repos identified

findings.text: Block explorer name-tag search yielded Euler Deployer on Base (0x95058f3d4c69f14f6125ad4602e925845bd5d6a4) and EUL token on Ethereum (0xd9fcd98c322942075a5c3860693e9f4f03aae07b). Further network-specific searches blocked by lack of programmatic explorer access.

Contract Addresses doc at https://docs.euler.finance/developers/contract-addresses/ designates https://github.com/euler-xyz/euler-interfaces as canonical source but page uses JavaScript dynamic loading for network tabs—addresses not statically present in fetched HTML.

29 audit reports completed by 12 firms (Spearbit, Certora, Hunter Security, Trail of Bits, Zellic, OpenZeppelin, Chain Security, Omniscia, yAudit, Ruptura, ChainSecurity, MixBytes). Over 60 total security reviews. Post-audit $1.25M Cantina competition found no medium+ severity issues. Security page confirms extensive audit corpus; individual PDF links not enumerated in fetched doc.

GitHub organization is https://github.com/euler-xyz with core repos: ethereum-vault-connector, euler-vault-kit, euler-price-oracle, euler-earn. Direct fetch of euler-interfaces blocked; repo references extracted from docs and searches.

16 chains confirmed in protocol.chains: Ethereum, Plasma, Avalanche, BOB, Monad, Hyperliquid L1, Arbitrum, Base, Unichain, Berachain, Binance, Linea, TAC, Sonic, Swellchain, Mantle. Per-chain address enumeration deferred to D1-D4 retries for each network.
Docs reference factory patterns (EulerSwap Factory for swap pools, vault factories for EVK-based vaults) but enumeration method not yet performed; capped at 50 per factory per protocol.

Role taxonomy identified from docs: owner, admin, proxy_admin, governor, timelock, guardian, multisig, treasury, oracle, factory, router, token, pool, vault, other. Core component roles: EVC (access-control), EVK (vault base), EPO (oracle), factories (pool/vault creation).
All non-dynamic sightings trace to fetched URLs. Dynamic addresses (loaded via JavaScript in Contract Addresses page and EUL Addresses page) cannot be catalogued without browser execution.

rationale.verdict: Address discovery for Euler V2 is constrained by dynamic loading on the canonical docs pages. Canonical sources (euler-interfaces repo, deployed-contracts doc) are identified but require either browser execution or direct repo access to enumerate. Core governance/admin addresses, token contract, and multi-chain deployment footprint are known to exist per linked audits and security docs, but per-chain admin/control addresses cannot be catalogued without executing the JavaScript-heavy docs site. Recommend next run inherit discovered source URLs and attempt fetch of raw GitHub JSON manifests if available.

evidence.shows: Official website with links to docs, GitHub org, forum, snapshot governance, and security/audit page
Canonical documentation site; links to developers section, security audits, EUL addresses, GitHub repos (EVC, EVK, EPO, Euler Earn), and contract addresses page

Designated contract addresses page; states addresses are fetched from https://github.com/euler-xyz/euler-interfaces/tree/master; uses JavaScript dynamic loading for network tabs—addresses not enumerated in static HTML
Links to audit pages for core protocol, Earn audits, Swap audits, Securitize audits; mentions extensive audit coverage and $1.25M Cantina competition post-deployment

EUL and rEUL addresses page; states addresses are in https://github.com/euler-xyz/euler-interfaces/tree/master/addresses grouped by network; uses JavaScript dynamic loading—addresses not present in static HTML

29 audit reports from 12 firms (Spearbit, Certora, Hunter Security, Trail of Bits, Zellic, OpenZeppelin, Chain Security, Omniscia, yAudit, Ruptura, ChainSecurity, MixBytes). Two critical and five high severity vulnerabilities found and resolved. $1.25M Cantina competition post-audit found no medium+ severity issues.
Euler bug bounty page ($7.5M total); states deployed vaults and contracts listed at Euler Docs Contract Addresses page; references 'verifiedArray() function of the perspective contracts'

Euler Labs GitHub organization. Repos include: ethereum-vault-connector, euler-vault-kit, euler-price-oracle, euler-earn, euler-verifier, euler-liquidation-bot, euler-subgraph, euler-tokenlist, and others.
Euler Deployer address on Base blockchain (0x95058f3d4c69f14f6125ad4602e925845bd5d6a4); holds 0.006803554309191924 ETH
EUL token contract on Ethereum (0xd9fcd98c322942075a5c3860693e9f4f03aae07b); ERC-20 with voting power and minting logic; treasury field present
OpenZeppelin audit of Ethereum Vault Connector (EVC); found issues related to input validation, security contacts, and pragma directives; demonstrates EVC as core audited component

Cantina Euler V2 competition page; lists audit reports from Certora, ChainSecurity, Hunter Security, Omniscia, OpenZeppelin, Spearbit, Trail of Bits, yAudit for EVC, EVK, and EPO with specific commit SHAs

unknowns: D1-offchain: Block explorer name-tag searches per chain (Plasma, Avalanche, BOB, Monad, Hyperliquid L1, Arbitrum, Base, Unichain, Berachain, Binance, Linea, TAC, Sonic, Swellchain, Mantle) blocked by lack of programmatic block-explorer API access; manual web_fetch of search handler pages blocked by dynamic rendering
D2-offchain: euler-interfaces repository direct fetch blocked; cannot enumerate addresses from github.com/euler-xyz/euler-interfaces/tree/master/addresses without browser execution
D4-offchain: GitHub deployment artifacts in euler-interfaces not accessible; Foundry broadcast/ and hardhat-deploy/ paths not enumerated
D6-offchain: Factory enumeration deferred; EulerSwap Factory and EVK vault factories require on-chain method calls (pools() / allPools / getVault) or subgraph queries
D5-offchain: Dynamic loading on Contract Addresses and EUL Addresses pages prevents static enumeration of per-chain addresses
D8-offchain: Cannot verify admin_addresses[] entries for any chain without executing dynamic JavaScript or accessing raw JSON from euler-interfaces repo

protocol_metadata.about: Euler V2 is a modular lending protocol featuring the Ethereum Vault Connector (EVC) for access control and vault coordination, the Euler Vault Kit (EVK) for creating customizable ERC-4626 lending vaults, and the Euler Price Oracle (EPO) for decentralized price feeds. The protocol enables users to lend, borrow, and swap assets across vaults with flexible collateral parameters and risk management. Deployed across 16 blockchain networks including Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, and others; governed by the EUL token through EulerDAO.
